title: clematis orientalis subsp. wightiana
intro: Clematis orientalis subsp. wightiana is a species of flowering plant in the buttercup family, native to India and Southeast Asia, known for its fragrant white flowers and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Clematis orientalis subsp. wightiana include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as promoting wound healing and soothing digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and decoctions, can be used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
